data.narrative:
Phaidra is a Series B artificial intelligence and software company founded by Jim Gao (former Google DeepMind industrial AI program lead), Vedavyas Panneershelvam (AlphaGo engineer at DeepMind), and Katherine Hoffman (operations, ex-Trane Technologies and Raytheon). The company has raised $120M to date. The peer universe comprises 7 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private comparables plus 1 public comparable (Palantir), with additional reference to frontier AI firms including Physical Intelligence, Black Forest Labs, Waymo, and Figure AI.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $120M total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a typical Series B to next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.9x, implies a valuation of $228M. Peer-median analysis across 7 AI & Software comparables at similar stages yields a median valuation of $14.0B, with a range spanning $3.2B (low) to $39.0B (high).

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $194M to $16.1B, with a median of $7.1B. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty regarding stage-specific pricing in the frontier AI market, where comparable valuations span two orders of magnitude. Key uncertainties include revenue traction relative to peers, technical defensibility of the core AI application, and the pace of commercialization relative to the company's burn rate and capital efficiency.
